reconciliation_notes.detail: Wizard step list is dynamic — 'Disclosure of data' only appears once sell/share=Yes OR CCPA thresholds=Yes OR Include-CCPA-clauses=Yes (cases 2, 48)
'Unsaved changes' confirmation dialog on step switch (cases 3-5), not documented in either spec PDF
'Resume your draft?' dialog on re-entry (cases 6-7), not documented
Entire 'Preview & add policy' step is undocumented in either spec PDF (case 36)

Three distinct plan-gate UX patterns coexist in this feature: disabled control + modal (cases 13/14/29), clickable chip + toast (case 21), visibly-disabled chip with inline Pro+ label (case 32) — neither doc distinguishes these
'Remove CCPA clauses?' / 'Remove GDPR clauses?' confirmation dialogs when toggling California/EU-EEA from Yes to No (cases 15-16, 45)
Direct-URL navigation between wizard steps bypasses in-page 'Next' validation entirely — used as a crawl workaround this session, not documented anywhere; worth flagging to the app team separately
Persistent 'fields not completely filled out' validation banner on Additional Information that did not clear even once every visible required field appeared filled — root cause unresolved (case 27)

'Preview Privacy Policy' is a separate popup distinct from the final 'Preview & add policy' step (case 41), plus an unsaved-primary-language-lost-on-refresh behavior (case 42), per the manually-authored test case file
A third distinct flow-control dialog: 'Back to Dashboard' -> 'Save progress?' (Discard/Save draft/Close), separate from the unsaved-changes and resume-draft dialogs (case 54)

CCPA 'users under 13' question and its Children's Privacy clause — Pro+-gated behind the unanswered for-profit question, never reached live; now covered via the manual test case file instead (case 48 area)
Secondary-language generation and the 'translations don't auto-update' caveat — only seen locked live, never exercised unlocked (case 40, doc-sourced)
'Categories sold or shared' field: both spec PDFs state a 500-char limit; live enforces 2000 chars via submit-time validation, not a native maxlength attribute (case 18, resolved)

UNRESOLVED CONFLICT: case 21 (live crawl) found CCPA-gated data-category chips render as plain, unlocked, clickable chips with a toast-on-click; case 49 (manual test case file, TC083-087) says these should render with a Pro+ badge, locked/disabled up front. Flagged for /grill-section to adjudicate, not resolved in this draft.
The character-limit doc flags roughly a dozen of its own current limits as wrong ('Increase'/'Add') — a pre-existing internal inconsistency in that source, not a live conflict

The standalone "Preview privacy policy" button (reachable from the Language preferences step at 0% wizard completion) renders the full policy translated into the current primary language using default content for unfilled sections. TC10 in PPG TESTCASES only checks that the popup opens/closes without errors — it doesn't mention this translation behavior at all.

The questionnaire doc marks both the DPO-contact and data-controller/representative fields on Miscellaneous disclosures 'Shown if: Users in EU/EEA = Yes'. Confirmed live that DPO correctly hides when EU/EEA is set to No, but the data-controller/representative field does not — it stays visible and editable, and its value is then silently dropped from the generated policy with no indication to the user.

The questionnaire doc's footnote ('H1, H2, H3, & H4 are set to 18, 16, 14, & 12 respectively, to understand the hierarchy') predicts a two-tier CCPA "Children's privacy" clause — ages 13–16 get explicit user consent, under 13 gets parental consent — that isn't documented anywhere in the testcases sheet. Confirmed live this pass.

A "Do we share your information?" GDPR sharing-basis section (three bullets: Sharing with your consent / Legal Obligations / For business transfers) renders in the generated policy whenever EU/EEA=Yes. Not documented in either source doc. Found during the Business Details matrix sweep (2026-07-15).

Resolves a previously-unconfirmed out_of_scope_note from the language-preferences pass: the questionnaire doc's 'Legal bases for collecting the data' EU/EEA=Yes gating claim is now confirmed live both directions — absent when EU/EEA=No, present when EU/EEA=Yes, alongside the under-16 age-gating question which is gated identically. Confirmed during the Business Details matrix sweep (2026-07-15).

First-pass text extraction of internal-docs/Character limit - Privacy Policy Fields (1).pdf (pdftotext -layout) appeared to show a CCPA-gated 'other purposes' question in Use of Data. Viewing the actual rendered PDF pages (5 and 12) resolved this: the CCPA condition belongs to a Disclosure of Data field ('Do you not sell or share data with any of the listed age groups?'), not to Use of Data's 'Are there any other purposes for which you use the collected data?' field, which has no gating condition at all. No real mismatch — resolved before writing any case, per migration-conventions.md §12's falsification-check discipline.
Doc does not mention duplicate-rejection or whitespace-only rejection behavior for the custom-purpose field; both confirmed live (duplicate silently not added, whitespace keeps Add disabled).

Data retention's 'Add your own retention period' free-text field: doc (internal-docs/Character limit - Privacy Policy Fields (1).pdf p.13, current-limit table) states the current character limit is 500. Live testing (typing progressively longer strings and reading the submit-time inline error) confirmed the actual live limit is 1000 — which matches the doc's own 'recommended' value on p.20, not its 'current' value. The doc's current-state column is stale; the recommendation appears to have already been implemented.

Data retention's 'Not yet decided' (Pro+) free-text field: doc (p.13) states the current character limit is 2000. Live testing confirmed the actual live limit is 2500 — again matching the doc's 'recommended' value (p.20) rather than its stated 'current' value. Same stale-doc pattern as the custom-period field above.

The 'Not yet decided' free-text field (only reachable on Pro+, since it's disabled on Free per existing case 39498) was never previously live-verified. Live confirms it renders a required field labeled 'Disclose how the retention period is determined.' — the doc (p.6, p.13) only names the field internally as 'not_yet_decided_additional' with a functional description ('Provide additional details about your data retention timeline'), not its exact live copy. Not a conflict, just previously unconfirmed.

Verified the 'Disclosure of data' step's full trigger rule live on Company details > Business details, on a clean rebuilt baseline (not the shared Pro account's pre-existing dirty draft state): EU/EEA=Yes alone never triggers it; California=Yes + for-profit=Yes alone (thresholds=No) is a confirmed no-op (matches existing case 39611); the step only appears once California=Yes AND (sell/share=Yes OR thresholds=Yes OR the 'Do you want to include CCPA-compliant clauses even if not legally required?' opt-in=Yes). That opt-in question — referenced only by case 39611's title, never walked through before — is a plain Business-details Yes/No question (not a modal) that only renders once for-profit=Yes and thresholds=No, and toggles the step on/off live with no confirmation dialog (unlike the EU/EEA and California toggles, which prompt 'Remove GDPR/CCPA clauses from your policy?' first). Not part of the internal docs at all; confirmed by direct live testing.

Disclosure of data's four field-level free-text limits (age-category opt-in/opt-out under 16 and under 13, the delete/correct/access process field, the sensitive-info-exemptions reveal, and both third-party-past-12-months reveal fields) all doc-state a current limit of 2000 (internal-docs/Character limit - Privacy Policy Fields (1).pdf p.12). Live testing (submit-time inline error) confirms the actual limit is 2500 across every one of them — matching the doc's own 'recommended' value (p.19) rather than its 'current' value. Same stale-doc-current-column pattern already found on Data Retention's fields.

The doc's field-to-question map (p.12) predicts the 'Do you disclose sensitive information for purposes beyond listed exemptions?' = Yes path reveals two fields (categories disclosed + third parties shared with), mirroring the 'disclosed to third parties in the past 12 months' = Yes path. Live only reveals one field: 'Describe the purposes for which you disclose Sensitive Personal Information beyond the listed exemptions.' The third-party-past-12-months path does match the doc's two-field prediction.

The 'Do you sell or share the personal information of users in the following categories?' question's three options (Users under 16 / Users under 13 / None) are a mutually-exclusive accordion, confirmed by direct testing (selecting one option collapses whichever was previously expanded and clears its fields from view). Neither doc states this. Also confirmed the under-13 opt-in/opt-out field wording explicitly references parental/guardian consent ('How can parents or guardians of such users opt in to a sale?'), while the under-16 wording does not ('What is the process by which such users can opt in to such a sale?') — a genuine content difference between the two brackets, not just a label swap.

DPO contact-details and data controller/representative fields: internal-docs/Character limit - Privacy Policy Fields (1).pdf p.13-14 states a current limit of 1000 for both; live enforces 2000, matching the doc's own recommended value (p.20-21) rather than its current column — same stale-doc-current-column pattern already found on Data Retention and Disclosure of Data fields.
Custom data-transfer-safeguards free-text field ('Add your own'): doc p.14 states current limit 2000; live enforces 3000, matching the doc's recommended value (p.21) — same stale-doc pattern.
CCPA metrics-link field: doc p.14 states current limit 500; live enforces 2000, matching the doc's recommended value (p.21) — same stale-doc pattern.

CCPA metrics-link field: doc p.14 marks this field 'Optional'; live requires it once 'Do you process the personal information of 10 million or more California residents?' = Yes, blocking Next with the standard 'fields not completely filled out' banner if left empty.

The 'What applicable safeguards have been implemented?' chip-select is multi-select, not mutually exclusive — confirmed live that 'Standard contractual clauses' and 'Add your own' can be selected simultaneously. Neither doc states this.

The 'Add your own' custom safeguard slot holds only one entry at a time — adding a new value replaces the existing custom chip rather than appending a second one. Neither doc states this, and it differs from other custom-entry lists elsewhere in the wizard (e.g. Use of data's custom-purpose list) which do accumulate multiple values.

Duplicate-value and whitespace-only rejection on the custom safeguard input (Add button stays disabled for whitespace; exact duplicates are silently not re-added) — same undocumented pattern already found on Data Retention's custom-period field.

Character-limit audit of Company Details, Collection of Data, and Use of Data (per BACKLOG.md's 'sweep the remaining PPG steps' item): Collection of Data's cookie policy URL field states a current limit of 500 (doc p.9-10); live enforces 2000, matching the doc's own recommended value (p.17, Assessment=Increase).
Collection of Data's 'Do Not Track' response field states a current limit of 2000 (doc p.10); live enforces 2500, matching the doc's own recommended value (p.17, Assessment=Increase).

Collection of Data's review/change-process description field (CalOPPA-gated, shown when sell/share=Yes) states a current limit of 2000 (doc p.11); live enforces 2500, matching the doc's own recommended value (p.18, Assessment=Increase).

cases.title: [Privacy Policy Generator] Verify that the wizard renders with all steps and a progress indicator
cases.preconditions: User is on the Dashboard for a website with no privacy policy created yet.

steps.content: Navigate to the Privacy Policy Generator entry point.
steps.expected: The wizard should open with heading "Privacy Policy" and subtext "Generating privacy policy for [website]."

steps.content: Observe the step sidebar.
steps.expected: A vertical list of steps should be displayed: Language preferences, Company details, Collection of data, Use of data, Data retention, Miscellaneous disclosures, Preview & add policy — all steps except Language preferences shown disabled until reached.

steps.content: Observe the progress indicator.
steps.expected: An "X% complete" label and progress bar should be displayed above the step content, updating as steps are completed (e.g. 20% after Collection of data, 100% at Preview & add policy).

steps.content: Observe the bottom action bar.
steps.expected: "Previous", "Save draft", and "Next" buttons should be displayed; the final step shows "Generate privacy policy" instead of "Next".

cases.rewrite_notes: confirmed via cumulative session evidence (2026-07-14) rather than a fresh no-policy account: heading/subtext, 7-step sidebar list (Disclosure of data correctly absent by default, matching case 2's OR-trigger), disabled-until-reached step gating, progress % updating (0/10/20/40/60/80/100 observed at various points), and the bottom action bar (Previous/Save draft/Next, final step showing "Generate privacy policy") were all directly observed at multiple points this session, including the very first Dashboard entry where later steps showed disabled.
cases.audit_note: Quoted text confirmed live word-for-word per rewrite_notes; no discrepancy from source found.

cases.title: [Privacy Policy Generator] Verify that "Discard changes" reverts unsaved edits when navigating away

cases.title: [Privacy Policy Generator] Verify that "Back to Dashboard" from any wizard step shows a Save Progress confirmation dialog
cases.preconditions: User is on any Privacy Policy Generator wizard step with entered or modified data that has not been saved.

steps.content: Click "Back to Dashboard".
steps.expected: A dialog titled "Save progress?" should appear with body text "Save a draft to continue where you left off when you return." and buttons "Discard", "Save draft" (plus a Close icon) — confirmed word-for-word live.

steps.content: Click "Discard".
steps.expected: The dialog should close and the user should be navigated to the Dashboard. [APP DEFECT — confirmed broad] On live re-test, Discard reverted the ENTIRE draft, not just the current step's unsaved edit: after discarding one unsaved DPO-field edit on Miscellaneous disclosures, Company details > Contact information (Name/Website/Email/URL-to-contact/Full address) came back completely blank, progress dropped to 10% complete, and the "Disclosure of data" step disappeared from the sidebar (sell/share=Yes reverted). This is a full-draft rollback to some earlier checkpoint, not a scoped undo of the current field — confirmed reproducible, root cause (which checkpoint it reverts to) not determinable from the UI alone; flag to engineering as a data-loss risk.

steps.content: Repeat and click "Save draft" instead.
steps.expected: A "Draft saved successfully!" message should be shown and the user should be navigated to the Dashboard.

steps.content: Repeat and click the Close (X) icon instead.
steps.expected: The dialog should close, the user should remain on the current wizard step, and all previously entered information should remain unchanged.

cases.rewrite_notes: dialog text/buttons confirmed live exactly (2026-07-14). Discard's over-broad revert is a real, reproduced concern — recorded as an open defect rather than silently treated as case-authoring error. Save-draft and Close-icon sub-paths not independently re-run this pass after the Discard finding; still need direct confirmation.
cases.audit_note: Dialog title/body/buttons confirmed live exactly, and the Discard over-broad-revert defect was independently reproduced — but per this case's own rewrite_notes, the "Draft saved successfully!" message (Save-draft sub-path, step 3) and the Close-icon sub-path (step 4) were "not independently re-run this pass after the Discard finding; still need direct confirmation." Needs a live check on those two remaining sub-paths.

cases.title: [Privacy Policy Generator] Verify that the custom data-transfer-safeguards field is multi-select, validates duplicates/whitespace/length, and holds only one custom entry
cases.preconditions:
- Miscellaneous disclosures step
- "Do you transfer data collected to a non-adequate country or region outside the EU?" = Yes
- "Standard contractual clauses" selected under "What applicable safeguards have been implemented?"

steps.content: Click "Add your own" without deselecting "Standard contractual clauses."
steps.expected: Both chips remain selected simultaneously — the safeguards field is multi-select, not mutually exclusive.

steps.content: Type a valid custom safeguard (e.g. "Data processed under an approved certification mechanism") into the field that appears and click "Add."
steps.expected: It's added as a chip with its own "Remove" control, shown below the chip-select options.

steps.content: Type the same value again and click "Add."
steps.expected: No duplicate chip is created; only one instance remains.

steps.content: Type only whitespace into the field.
steps.expected: The "Add" button stays disabled; no chip is added.

steps.content: Type a value exceeding 3000 characters and click "Add."
steps.expected: Inline error "Please limit your response to 3000 characters." is shown; no chip is added.

steps.content: Type a second, different valid value and click "Add."
steps.expected: The new value replaces the existing custom chip rather than being added alongside it — only one custom safeguard entry is retained at a time, unlike duplicate-entry chip lists used elsewhere in the wizard (e.g. Use of data's custom-purpose list).
